title: Need Help Finding Child Care? Call 211 - 211info
text: Did you know that 211info has a dedicated child care team who provide free, customized referrals for child care, preschool, behavioral programs, parenting classes, and other family support resources? “We aim to connect all families across Oregon – regardless of income – with programs that provide not just supervision, but also opportunities for enrichment and development for children of all ages,” says 211info Child Care Director, Kerry Wels. In partnership with the Oregon Department of Early Learning and Care (DELC) , 211info child care referral specialists help families navigate quality care options, and offer guidance on Employment Related Day Care (ERDC), Preschool Promise, Baby Promise and Head Start – including how they work and what’s needed to apply. They can also provide various other local resources to help with food, housing, utility assistance, and much more. To learn more, check out this video on how the 211info child care line works and why it’s important, from our partners at DELC. Ready to reach out? To speak with a child care referral specialist about child care resources, call 211. You can also text the keyword “children” or “ninos” to 898211or email [email protected] . To search online for child care providers in your area, visit findchildcareoregon.org . The 211info Child Care Team is available Monday through Friday, 7 AM – 11 PM and Saturday/Sunday, 8 AM – 8 PM to provide free, customized referrals to child care providers and strategies for finding quality child care in Oregon.
